mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hike periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the labor market exceeding 0.5 percentage points in the UNRATE over a three-month window signals impending recession risk, triggering institutional risk-off rebalancing that depresses equity prices over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal's predictive Rank IC drops below 0.02 across three consecutive rolling 12-month periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in residential construction permits and consumer credit balances indicates robust aggregate demand and healthy household balance sheets, which historically supports positive equity returns over a full quarter as earnings growth accelerates.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative directional accuracy below 45% over a 5-year out-of-sample test, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5% year-over-year, rising industrial production demonstrates supply-side resilience that offsets monetary tightening, leading to positive short-term equity performance as corporate margins stabilize.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns turn negative in more than 60% of high-inflation, rising-INDPRO instances, or the signal's information ratio falls below 0.1 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth falling below 1.5% confirms an economic slowdown that reduces corporate revenue expectations, driving sustained selling pressure that depresses S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth rate falls below 1.5%
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if 60-day forward returns remain positive in over 50% of sub-1.5% GDP growth episodes, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC consistently exceeds 0.05 in the opposite direction.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls sustains household disposable income and consumer spending power, providing a stable earnings floor that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward directional accuracy falls below 50% over any 4-year period, or the signal exhibits no statistical edge (t-statistic < 1.0) when compared to a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL rises faster than FEDFUNDS increases, the resulting decline in real interest rates historically triggers equity sell-offs as margin compression accelerates and input costs outpace pricing power, depressing 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction signal yields a positive predictive accuracy above 55% over a 5-year out-of-sample test, or the joint coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: In an accommodative monetary policy environment where FEDFUNDS remains below 2.0%, housing starts exceeding 1250 thousand units signal strong credit availability and wealth effects that lift equity prices over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns turn negative in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the signal's predictive Rank IC drops below 0.02 across three consecutive rolling 12-month periods.
